root_text.an6.18:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosalesu cārikaṁ carati mahatā bhikkhusaṅghena saddhiṁ.
root_text.an6.18:1.2: Addasā kho bhagavā addhānamaggappaṭipanno aññatarasmiṁ padese macchikaṁ macchabandhaṁ macche vadhitvā vadhitvā vikkiṇamānaṁ.
root_text.an6.18:1.3: Disvā maggā okkamma aññatarasmiṁ rukkhamūle paññatte āsane nisīdi.
root_text.an6.18:1.5: “passatha no tumhe, bhikkhave, amuṁ macchikaṁ macchabandhaṁ macche vadhitvā vadhitvā vikkiṇamānan”ti?
root_text.an6.18:2.2: api nu tumhehi diṭṭhaṁ vā sutaṁ vā:
root_text.an6.18:2.3: ‘macchiko macchabandho macche vadhitvā vadhitvā vikkiṇamāno tena kammena tena ājīvena hatthiyāyī vā assayāyī vā rathayāyī vā yānayāyī vā bhogabhogī vā mahantaṁ vā bhogakkhandhaṁ ajjhāvasanto’”ti?
root_text.an6.18:2.6: Mayāpi kho etaṁ, bhikkhave, neva diṭṭhaṁ na sutaṁ:
root_text.an6.18:2.7: ‘macchiko macchabandho macche vadhitvā vadhitvā vikkiṇamāno tena kammena tena ājīvena hatthiyāyī vā assayāyī vā rathayāyī vā yānayāyī vā bhogabhogī vā mahantaṁ vā bhogakkhandhaṁ ajjhāvasanto’ti.
root_text.an6.18:2.9: Te hi so, bhikkhave, macche vajjhe vadhāyupanīte pāpakena manasānupekkhati, tasmā so neva hatthiyāyī hoti na assayāyī na rathayāyī na yānayāyī na bhogabhogī, na mahantaṁ bhogakkhandhaṁ ajjhāvasati.
root_text.an6.18:3.2: api nu tumhehi diṭṭhaṁ vā sutaṁ vā:
root_text.an6.18:3.3: ‘goghātako gāvo vadhitvā vadhitvā vikkiṇamāno tena kammena tena ājīvena hatthiyāyī vā assayāyī vā rathayāyī vā yānayāyī vā bhogabhogī vā mahantaṁ vā bhogakkhandhaṁ ajjhāvasanto’”ti?
root_text.an6.18:3.6: Mayāpi kho etaṁ, bhikkhave, neva diṭṭhaṁ na sutaṁ:
root_text.an6.18:3.7: ‘goghātako gāvo vadhitvā vadhitvā vikkiṇamāno tena kammena tena ājīvena hatthiyāyī vā assayāyī vā rathayāyī vā yānayāyī vā bhogabhogī vā mahantaṁ vā bhogakkhandhaṁ ajjhāvasanto’ti.
root_text.an6.18:3.9: Te hi so, bhikkhave, gāvo vajjhe vadhāyupanīte pāpakena manasānupekkhati, tasmā so neva hatthiyāyī hoti na assayāyī na rathayāyī na yānayāyī na bhogabhogī, na mahantaṁ bhogakkhandhaṁ ajjhāvasati.
root_text.an6.18:4.2: api nu tumhehi diṭṭhaṁ vā sutaṁ vā:
root_text.an6.18:4.6: māgaviko mage vadhitvā vadhitvā vikkiṇamāno tena kammena tena ājīvena hatthiyāyī vā assayāyī vā rathayāyī vā yānayāyī vā bhogabhogī vā mahantaṁ vā bhogakkhandhaṁ ajjhāvasanto’”ti?
root_text.an6.18:4.9: Mayāpi kho etaṁ, bhikkhave, neva diṭṭhaṁ na sutaṁ:
root_text.an6.18:4.10: ‘māgaviko mage vadhitvā vadhitvā vikkiṇamāno tena kammena tena ājīvena hatthiyāyī vā assayāyī vā rathayāyī vā yānayāyī vā bhogabhogī vā mahantaṁ vā bhogakkhandhaṁ ajjhāvasanto’ti.
root_text.an6.18:4.12: Te hi so, bhikkhave, mage vajjhe vadhāyupanīte pāpakena manasānupekkhati, tasmā so neva hatthiyāyī hoti na assayāyī na rathayāyī na yānayāyī na bhogabhogī, na mahantaṁ bhogakkhandhaṁ ajjhāvasati.
root_text.an6.18:4.13: Te hi nāma so, bhikkhave, tiracchānagate pāṇe vajjhe vadhāyupanīte pāpakena manasānupekkhamāno neva hatthiyāyī bhavissati na assayāyī na rathayāyī na yānayāyī na bhogabhogī, na mahantaṁ bhogakkhandhaṁ ajjhāvasissati.
root_text.an6.18:4.14: Ko pana vādo yaṁ manussabhūtaṁ vajjhaṁ vadhāyupanītaṁ pāpakena manasānupekkhati.
root_text.an6.18:4.15: Tañhi tassa, bhikkhave, hoti dīgharattaṁ ahitāya dukkhāya.
root_text.an6.18:4.16: Kāyassa bhedā paraṁ maraṇā apāyaṁ duggatiṁ vinipātaṁ nirayaṁ upapajjatī”ti.

translation_text.an6.18:1.1: At one time the Buddha was wandering in the land of the Kosalans together with a large Saṅgha of mendicants.
translation_text.an6.18:1.2: While walking along the road he saw a fish dealer in a certain spot selling fish that he had killed himself.
translation_text.an6.18:1.3: Seeing this he left the road, sat at the root of a tree on the seat spread out,
translation_text.an6.18:1.5: “Mendicants, do you see that fish dealer selling fish that he killed himself?”
translation_text.an6.18:2.2: Have you ever seen or heard
translation_text.an6.18:2.3: of a fish dealer selling fish that he killed himself who, by means of that work and livelihood, got to travel by elephant, horse, chariot, or vehicle, or to enjoy wealth, or to live off a large fortune?”
translation_text.an6.18:2.6: I too have never seen or heard of such a thing.
translation_text.an6.18:2.9: Because when the fish are led to the slaughter he regards them with bad intentions.
translation_text.an6.18:3.2: Have you ever seen or heard
translation_text.an6.18:3.3: of a butcher of cattle selling cattle that he killed himself who, by means of that work and livelihood, got to travel by elephant, horse, chariot, or vehicle, or to enjoy wealth, or to live off a large fortune?”
translation_text.an6.18:3.6: I too have never seen or heard of such a thing.
translation_text.an6.18:3.9: Because when the cattle are led to the slaughter he regards them with bad intentions.
translation_text.an6.18:4.2: Have you ever seen or heard
translation_text.an6.18:4.6: or a deer-hunter selling deer which he killed himself who, by means of that work and livelihood, got to travel by elephant, horse, chariot, or vehicle, or to enjoy wealth, or to live off a large fortune?”
translation_text.an6.18:4.9: I too have never seen or heard of such a thing.
translation_text.an6.18:4.12: Because when the deer are led to the slaughter he regards them with bad intentions.
translation_text.an6.18:4.13: By regarding even animals led to the slaughter with bad intentions he did not get to travel by elephant, horse, chariot, or vehicle, or to enjoy wealth, or to live off a large fortune.
translation_text.an6.18:4.14: How much worse is someone who regards human beings brought to the slaughter with bad intentions!
translation_text.an6.18:4.15: This will be for their lasting harm and suffering.
translation_text.an6.18:4.16: When their body breaks up, after death, they’re reborn in a place of loss, a bad place, the underworld, hell.”

variant_text.an6.18:2.9: vadhāyupanīte → vadhāyopanīte (bj); vadhāyānīte (sya-all, km); vadhāya nīte (mr)
variant_text.an6.18:4.13: nāma → idaṁ padaṁ bahūsu, manasānupekkhamāno → tasmā so (sya-all, mr) | bhavissati → hoti (sya-all, mr) | ajjhāvasissati → ajjhāvasati (sya-all, mr)
